macro_summary: The S&P 500 closed Friday at 744.78 with post-holiday futures pointing to a steady open. The dollar slipped 0.58% for the week after June payrolls missed badly (57k vs 129k), collapsing rate-hike bets and sending gold above $4,200. Oil is drifting near $68 as the Hormuz de-escalation continues, though peace talks are now on hold.
commodities_note: WTI ended Friday at $68.65, down 28.5% in a month as OPEC+ output rebounds and the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Gold rallied 3% on the week to $4,201 — safe-haven demand persists despite the ceasefire. US10Y held at 4.49%, still well above the 4.20% threshold that would ease Singapore bank NIM compression.

WeRide bull case rests on B2B fleet licensing (Uber, Grab, Bosch) + multi-country Robotaxi deployment — evaluate on commercial traction, not autonomy benchmarks vs Waymo/Tesla.
ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

writeup.headline: Speculative robotaxi play — R:R 2.1:1 derived, but ADR risk keeps it in the penalty box.
writeup.what_to_do: WRD's Uber/Grab robotaxi partnerships are progressing (Zurich launch, per Insider Monkey), but the ADR delisting risk is a standing invalidator. R:R 2.1:1 is derived from estimated support, not observed. Avoid until a China delisting resolution or a named fleet-scale deal with a dollar figure.

SITM.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 56x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

writeup.headline: MEMS timing is a structural growth story, but -12% below SMA50 with 56x PE blocks entry.
writeup.what_to_do: SITM's MEMS timing displacement of quartz is a multi-year thesis, but the stock is -12% below its 50-day, forward PE 56x, and R:R 1.7:1. No entry until a named hyperscaler design win validates the AI timing revenue ramp.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 56x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

singapore.summary: All three banks are hard-blocked on extension with RSI above 65. Dual NIM compression (US rates + MAS S$NEER) persists; OCBC is the relative profit outperformer but no entry until US10Y breaks below 4.20% or the October MAS decision approaches.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — awaiting NIM relief
singapore.key_development: US10Y at 4.49% remains well above the 4.20% threshold needed for NIM relief; MAS S$NEER appreciation pace unchanged since April.

semiconductors.summary: Semis cluster is under heavy pressure: SOXX fell 9.4% last week as Meta's AI cloud entry amplifies capex ROI fears. NVDA holds above the 200-day but volume is light; TSEM and INTC are now AVOID on thesis impairments. TSM's CoWoS bottleneck remains the cleanest structural play into July 16 earnings.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — capex confirmed but ROI fears dominate
semiconductors.key_development: Meta's AI cloud business disclosure (per Yahoo) deepened hyperscaler peak fears, crushing SOXX -5.6% on Friday.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is in a holding pattern. MSFT is blocked on a 5-day surge; AMZN and GOOG are near their 50-day lines but lack catalysts for an upgrade. OpenAI's $21B loss (per Forbes) casts a shadow over Azure's AI revenue quality, but non-OpenAI RPO growth remains the clean signal.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautious — AI spend heavy, ROI narrative not yet proven
big_tech.key_development: Meta's neocloud ambitions (per Investing.com) threaten AWS/Azure/Google Cloud pricing; the competitive landscape is intensifying.

interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after NVDA's next networking product cycle update for Switch fabric demand.

active_risks: Hormuz disruption: US-Iran peace talks on hold; Strait throughput at 3.8 mbpd (vs 20-21 mbpd pre-war) but markets pricing surplus as OPEC+ output surges.
US-China tech tensions: Alibaba bans Anthropic's Claude Code (per Reuters) amid AI distillation spat; Intel restarts older CPU supply to China, signalling partial de-escalation.
USMCA tariff review: Trump administration opts for annual reviews, not a long-term deal; manufacturers with cross-border exposure face policy uncertainty.
Global trade fragmentation: Brazil pushes back on proposed 25% US tariffs; EU steel and e-commerce rules tighten, adding friction.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $68.65 (-28.5% monthly) discounts a full Hormuz reopening; energy stocks are not pricing in disruption. Semis remain under macro pressure from AI capex ROI fears and US-China export controls, keeping NVDA and TSM in WATCH territory. Singapore banks await NIM relief from the Fed.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US-Iran peace talks stalled (per CNBC); Alibaba restricts employees from using Anthropic's Claude Code (per Reuters).

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, WTI stays near $68; semis grind sideways as AI capex fears persist. Singapore banks remain extended but NIM relief catalysts fade into October MAS decision.

optimistic.description: US-Iran peace deal formalised, Strait fully reopens; WTI drops to $60, lifting consumer sentiment and easing inflation. Semis rally as capex ROI fears ease; NVDA and TSM re-rate higher.

pessimistic.description: Peace talks collapse, Hormuz fully closed again; WTI spikes back above $90, deepening inflation and pushing Fed hikes. Semis sell off sharply on macro and supply chain fears; Singapore banks NIM compression worsens.

wildcard.description: China-Philippines Sabina Shoal escalation triggers broader Pacific conflict, disrupting global shipping routes; energy and shipping stocks surge while tech supply chains freeze.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash, no new entries in extended semis or Singapore banks; await consolidation or macro catalyst confirmation before deploying capital.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautious — 8 of 29 names hard-blocked on extension
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 8 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension (DBS, OCBC, UOB, AMD, MU, SK Hynix, MSFT, PLTR). The macro backdrop is shifting dovish on payrolls, but the AI capex ROI debate and Hormuz tail-risk keep the overall posture defensive.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt.
